title: Clarify let _ = der pattern in config.rs
description: config.rs auth_provider() binds 'der' from ca_cert_bytes and then immediately discards it with 'let _ = der' in two places (lines 78 and 91). A contributor reading this will be confused: why bind it only to drop it? This is a compiler-appeasing pattern (to consume the Option so it doesn't fall through to the CustomCaAuth branch below), but without a comment it looks like a bug or dead code. Add a brief comment such as '// consumed here; --ca-cert is incompatible with header-based auth' to make the intent clear, or restructure the control flow so the discard is unnecessary.
close_reason: Stale: let _ = der pattern does not exist in config.rs; DER bytes are named der and passed directly to CustomCaAuth::new
